# Create a 3D prediction surface

Generates an Estimated Marginal Surface (EMS) of model predictions
across two continuous focal variables. Non-focal variables are
marginalised (held at mean/mode or averaged). Optionally stratified by a
categorical variable to produce overlaid surfaces.

## Usage

``` r
surf_prediction(
  model,
  x,
  y,
  by = NULL,
  x_range = NULL,
  y_range = NULL,
  x_length = 50L,
  y_length = 50L,
  ci = TRUE,
  level = 0.95,
  grid_type = "mean_or_mode",
  at = NULL,
  levels_needed = NULL,
  transform = NULL,
  labels = list(),
  smooth = NULL,
  predict_fun = NULL,
  method = c("marginaleffects", "emmeans", "gratia", "manual"),
  ...
)
```

## Arguments

- model:

  A fitted model object. Supports 100+ model classes via
  `marginaleffects`, including `lm`, `glm`, `gam`, `bam`, `lmer`,
  `glmer`, `brm`, and many more.

- x:

  Character. Name of the x-axis continuous variable.

- y:

  Character. Name of the y-axis continuous variable.

- by:

  Character or `NULL`. Name of a categorical variable for
  stratification. Each level produces a separate surface.

- x_range:

  Numeric vector of length 2. Range `c(min, max)` for x. If `NULL`,
  auto-detected from the model's training data.

- y_range:

  Numeric vector of length 2. Range `c(min, max)` for y. If `NULL`,
  auto-detected.

- x_length:

  Integer. Number of grid points along x. Default 50.

- y_length:

  Integer. Number of grid points along y. Default 50.

- ci:

  Logical. Whether to compute confidence intervals. Default `TRUE`.

- level:

  Numeric. Confidence level. Default `0.95`.

- grid_type:

  Character. How to handle non-focal variables. One of `"mean_or_mode"`
  (default) or `"counterfactual"`. See
  [`marginaleffects::datagrid()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/marginaleffects/man/datagrid.html)
  for details.

- at:

  Named list. Fix specific variables at given values (e.g.,
  `at = list(state = "WA", nitrogen = 80)`).

- levels_needed:

  Character vector or `NULL`. Subset the `by` variable to specific
  levels.

- transform:

  Character, function, or `NULL`. Back-transformation for the response
  (e.g., `"expit"` for logit-scale models, `"exp"` for log-scale).
  Built-in options: `"expit"`, `"exp"`, `"square"`, `"percent_expit"`.
  Or supply a custom function.

- labels:

  Named list with custom axis labels: `x`, `y`, `z`, `title`.

- smooth:

  Post-prediction surface smoothing via
  [`mgcv::gam()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/mgcv/man/gam.html). Useful for
  non-smooth models (random forests, boosted trees, MARS) where raw
  predictions form jagged or step-function surfaces. A tensor product
  smooth `te(x, y)` is fitted to the predicted values as a visual
  approximation. Options:

  - `NULL` (default): No smoothing – raw predictions displayed.

  - `TRUE`: Auto-smooth with `te(x, y, k = -1, bs = "tp")`, letting
    `mgcv` auto-select the basis dimension (same default as
    [`mgcv::s()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/mgcv/man/s.html)).

  - A named list for manual control:

    - `k`: Basis dimension per marginal (default `-1` = auto). Higher
      values preserve more detail; lower values smooth more.

    - `bs`: Basis type (default `"tp"`). See
      [`mgcv::te()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/mgcv/man/te.html).

    - `smooth_ci`: Logical. Also smooth confidence intervals? Default
      `TRUE`. When strata are present (`by` is set), a separate smooth
      is fitted per stratum, preserving interaction structure across
      groups. Requires the `mgcv` package.

- predict_fun:

  Custom prediction function with signature `function(model, newdata)`.
  Bypasses `marginaleffects`.

- method:

  Character. Computation backend: `"marginaleffects"` (default),
  `"gratia"` (for mgcv models), or `"manual"` (fallback).

- ...:

  Additional arguments passed to the prediction backend.

## Value

An
[effectsurf](https://aagi-aus.github.io/effectsurf/reference/new_effectsurf.md)
object. Use [`plot()`](https://rdrr.io/r/graphics/plot.default.html) to
visualise,
[`surf_data()`](https://aagi-aus.github.io/effectsurf/reference/surf_data.md)
to extract the prediction grid.

## See also

[`plot.effectsurf()`](https://aagi-aus.github.io/effectsurf/reference/plot.effectsurf.md),
[`surf_comparison()`](https://aagi-aus.github.io/effectsurf/reference/surf_comparison.md),
[`surf_slopes()`](https://aagi-aus.github.io/effectsurf/reference/surf_slopes.md),
[`surf_profile()`](https://aagi-aus.github.io/effectsurf/reference/surf_profile.md),
[`surf_export()`](https://aagi-aus.github.io/effectsurf/reference/surf_export.md)

## Examples

``` r
# \donttest{
if (requireNamespace("mgcv", quietly = TRUE)) {
  # Fit a GAM
  model <- mgcv::gam(
    mpg ~ s(wt) + s(hp) + factor(cyl),
    data = mtcars
  )

  # Basic prediction surface
  es <- surf_prediction(model, x = "wt", y = "hp")

  # Stratified by cylinder count
  es_strat <- surf_prediction(model, x = "wt", y = "hp",
                              by = "cyl",
                              x_length = 30, y_length = 30)

  # Plot interactively
  plot(es_strat)
}
```
